get.description: Get run logs of a given task by its execution ID (also referred to as run ID)

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of log entries to return. The default value is 50 and the maximum value is 200

schema.description: Number of log entries to return. The default value is 50 and the maximum value is 200

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: latest date and time of the logs to be retrieved. By default, current time is used

schema.description: latest date and time of the logs to be retrieved. By default, current time is used

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The way in which the returned logs are sorted by their timestamps. The default value is DESC, meaning the latest logs are put first

schema.description: The way in which the returned logs are sorted by their timestamps. The default value is DESC, meaning the latest logs are put first

parameters.name: startIndex
parameters.description: Start position of the pagination of the logs, greater or equal to 0

schema.description: Start position of the pagination of the logs, greater or equal to 0

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run

schema.description: Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run

size.description: Number of log entries in the current page

totalSize.description: Total number of log entries for the give execution ID

totalSizeFiltered.description: Total number of log entries for the given execution ID, for the specific time range defned in the request

data.description: List of execution logs. Log messages are in raw format, including information about severity, message, timestamps and so on.

nextIndex.description: Next index for pagination if it exists

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

post.summary: Generate logs for a completed or ongoing execution
post.description:
This endpoint generates the log of a given task run and returns a download token for this log (maximum log size: 100,000).

This token is valid for one hour.    

If the desired log size exceeds the 100,000-entry limit, use the time range parameters (`startTime` or `endTime` or both) to split the requests. Adjust the time range for each request to retrieve all log entries by making multiple calls.    
For example, use `endTime` in each request to retrieve up to 100,000 log entries before that time. Repeat with earlier `endTime` values until you have retrieved the desired log history.

parameters.name: fileFormat
parameters.description: Format of the log file to be generated. The available formats are `json` and `text`.

schema.description: Format of the log file to be generated. The available formats are `json` and `text`.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Latest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, current time is used

schema.description: Latest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, current time is used

parameters.name: newFileName
parameters.description:
This parameter indicates whether you need to include task IDs and execution IDs in the filename of the log to be generated. It generates a distinctive file name reading like: `taskID_executionID.log`

It is recommended to set this value to be true as the old filename format is going to be deprecated.

schema.description:
This parameter indicates whether you need to include task IDs and execution IDs in the filename of the log to be generated. It generates a distinctive file name reading like: `taskID_executionID.log`

It is recommended to set this value to be true as the old filename format is going to be deprecated.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run

schema.description: Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run

token.description: Token used to check the status of the log file generation

206.description: The downloaded logs contain only partial content due to the maximum log size limit of 100,000 entries.

token.description: Token used to check the status of the log file generation

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

delete.summary: Delete logs of a specified execution
delete.description: This endpoint allows users with the `Execution Logs - Delete` permission  (ID: `EXECUTION_LOGS_DELETE`) to delete logs for a given execution identified by its ID.

200.description: The logs have been deleted successfully or there are no logs to be deleted.

400.description: execution ID is not a UUID

404.description: Execution with provided ID, has not been found

500.description: Something went wrong and some or none of the execution logs have been deleted

post.summary: Check log generation status and return download URL
post.description:
Check the status of the generated log and return a download URL.

You need to put the download token in the body.

requestBody.description: Token returned to the request at the POST /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs endpoint.

schema.description: Token returned to the request at the POST /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs endpoint.

presignedURL.description: URL to download full logs, valid for 60 minutes

status.description: Status of full log file generation

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

Public.description: This Bearer authentication can be set in the Authorization header of your requests. Authentication tokens and Personal Access Tokens are supported. Personal Access Tokens can be generated in the Profile Preferences page of the Talend Cloud Portal.

Logs.description: Retrieve logs of your completed and ongoing executions.
